mcq.question: What is an interrupt?
mcq.correct: A signal to the CPU requiring immediate attention
wrong: A section of memory used by the CPU
A system for storing program data
A set of instructions written in code

mcq.question: What does the CPU do when an interrupt occurs?
mcq.correct: Pauses the current task and processes the interrupt
wrong: Ignores the interrupt and continues processing
Immediately stops all processing permanently
Saves the program and shuts down

mcq.question: What is an ISR?
mcq.correct: A program that runs when an interrupt occurs
wrong: A section of memory used during execution
A type of high-speed cache memory
A method used to send data across networks

mcq.question: Which of these is a hardware interrupt?
wrong: A calculation error during execution
A program requesting access to memory
An instruction being processed by the CPU

mcq.question: Which of these is a software interrupt?
wrong: Mouse input from a user
A printer running out of paper
A key being pressed on the keyboard

mcq.question: What happens after the Interrupt Service Routine is completed?
mcq.correct: The CPU resumes the original process
wrong: The CPU starts a new unrelated process
The current program is permanently stopped
The system returns to the start of execution

mcq.question: Why are interrupts useful?
mcq.correct: They allow quick response to important events
wrong: They increase the amount of data stored
They reduce the size of files in memory
They improve how images are displayed
